config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menghapus martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang bisa menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan satu sepenuhnya di atas yang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of statement sounds dangerous because it can be used to excuse government overreach and abuse, especially against unpopular minorities. I do think there are times when public safety and shared responsibility matter, but those limits should be narrow and justified, not a blank check to override basic rights. A healthy society protects people by respecting individual rights first, then balancing them carefully when there is a real and proven need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia pun kepentingan masyarakat sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan kebebasan warga, padahal yang paling rentan justru individu atau kelompok kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak individu bisa disapu begitu saja, itu membuka pintu bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama mayoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dan adil di antara keduanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From a United States perspective, I am wary of any argument that lets the majority or the government decide that some people can be sacrificed for the "greater good," because that has historically been used to justify discrimination and abuse. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be pursued within firm limits that protect speech, due process, privacy, and equal treatment. To me, a society is strongest when it is constrained by individual rights, not when it overrides them whenever that seems convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan terhadap warga, terutama kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Bagi saya, hak individu bukan penghalang bagi kebaikan bersama, melainkan batas agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena atas nama ketertiban atau moralitas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar sesuka hati. Jadi, yang harus diprioritaskan bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan perlindungan hak individu sambil mencari kepentingan bersama secara seimbang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that idea is too easy to abuse because “society” often ends up meaning the majority, the government, or whoever has power at the moment, and history shows that minorities pay the price. I do think there are rare cases where individual rights are limited for real public necessity, but those exceptions should be narrow, transparent, and accountable, not treated as the default. To me, a free society is one that starts by protecting rights and only then asks how to serve the common good within those limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
